Operation 'stopModel' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'StopModel' is a generic operation name, not an action-oriented description. The description lacks clarity about: (1) what 'hosting' means in this context (inference endpoints), (2) explicit side effects (endpoints become unavailable), (3) the asynchronous nature and expected duration, (4) what the 202 response indicates. Improved version clarifies the action, expected inputs (project and model version), state transitions, side effects, and response semantics for better AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

data.current_description: Stops the hosting of a running model. The operation might take a while to complete. To check the current status, call DescribeModel. After the model hosting stops, the Status of the model is TRAINED. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:StopModel operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Stop hosting a running model
data.description_suggestion: Stops hosting a running model for the specified project and model version. This is an asynchronous operation that may take several minutes to complete. Upon successful completion, the model status transitions to TRAINED and inference endpoints become unavailable. Use DescribeModel to poll the current status. Requires lookoutvision:StopModel IAM permission. Returns a 202 Accepted response indicating the stop request has been initiated.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'StopModel' is a generic operation name, not an action-oriented description. The description lacks clarity about: (1) what 'hosting' means in this context (inference endpoints), (2) explicit side effects (endpoints become unavailable), (3) the asynchronous nature and expected duration, (4) what the 202 response indicates. Improved version clarifies the action, expected inputs (project and model version), state transitions, side effects, and response semantics for better AI agent decision-making about when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'createDataset' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, not_actionable. Summary is a generic operation name rather than an action-oriented statement. The description is comprehensive and clear about inputs (DatasetType, DatasetSource), outputs, and usage patterns. Improved summary uses action verb and specifies the dataset types that can be created.

data.current_description: Creates a new dataset in an Amazon Lookout for Vision project. CreateDataset can create a training or a test dataset from a valid dataset source (DatasetSource). If you want a single dataset project, specify train for the value of DatasetType. To have a project with separate training and test datasets, call CreateDataset twice. On the first call, specify train for the value of DatasetType. On the second call, specify test for the value of DatasetType. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:CreateDataset operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Create training or test dataset for project
data.reasoning: Summary is a generic operation name rather than an action-oriented statement. The description is comprehensive and clear about inputs (DatasetType, DatasetSource), outputs, and usage patterns. Improved summary uses action verb and specifies the dataset types that can be created.

message: Operation 'createModel' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, not_actionable. Summary is a generic operation name. The description is well-structured and explains the asynchronous nature, dataset handling, status checking via DescribeModel, output location, and permission requirements. Improved summary uses action verbs and clarifies the core operations (training and evaluation).

data.current_description: Creates a new version of a model within an an Amazon Lookout for Vision project. CreateModel is an asynchronous operation in which Amazon Lookout for Vision trains, tests, and evaluates a new version of a model. To get the current status, check the Status field returned in the response from DescribeModel. If the project has a single dataset, Amazon Lookout for Vision internally splits the dataset to create a training and a test dataset. If the project has a training and a test dataset, Lookout for Vision uses the respective datasets to train and test the model. After training completes, the evaluation metrics are stored at the location specified in OutputConfig. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:CreateModel operation. If you want to tag your model, you also require permission to the lookoutvision:TagResource operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Train and evaluate new model version
data.reasoning: Summary is a generic operation name. The description is well-structured and explains the asynchronous nature, dataset handling, status checking via DescribeModel, output location, and permission requirements. Improved summary uses action verbs and clarifies the core operations (training and evaluation).

The description comprehensively explains side effects and constraints for both single and dual dataset projects, which is valuable for AI agents. data.current_description: Deletes an existing Amazon Lookout for Vision dataset. If your the project has a single dataset, you must create a new dataset before you can create a model. If you project has a training dataset and a test dataset consider the following. If you delete the test dataset, your project reverts to a single dataset project. If you then train the model, Amazon Lookout for Vision internally splits the remaining dataset into a training and test dataset. If you delete the training dataset, you must create a training dataset before you can create a model. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:DeleteDataset operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Delete dataset from project
data.reasoning: Summary is a generic operation name. The description comprehensively explains side effects and constraints for both single and dual dataset projects, which is valuable for AI agents. Improved summary uses action verb and specifies what is being deleted.

data.current_description: Detects anomalies in an image that you supply. The response from DetectAnomalies includes a boolean prediction that the image contains one or more anomalies and a confidence value for the prediction. If the model is an image segmentation model, the response also includes segmentation information for each type of anomaly found in the image. Before calling DetectAnomalies, you must first start your model with the StartModel operation. You are charged for the amount of time, in minutes, that a model runs and for the number of anomaly detection units that your model uses. If you are not using a model, use the StopModel operation to stop your model. For more information, see Detecting anomalies in an image in the Amazon Lookout for Vision developer guide. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:DetectAnomalies operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Detect anomalies in an image using a model
data.reasoning: Summary 'DetectAnomalies' is a generic operation name lacking context about inputs and outputs. Improved summary clarifies that an image is analyzed using a model. message: Operation 'updateDatasetEntries' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary 'UpdateDatasetEntries' is not action-oriented. Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity about the response (202 Accepted) and could better emphasize the replacement behavior and annotation removal side effects for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Adds or updates one or more JSON Line entries in a dataset. A JSON Line includes information about an image used for training or testing an Amazon Lookout for Vision model. To update an existing JSON Line, use the source-ref field to identify the JSON Line. The JSON line that you supply replaces the existing JSON line. Any existing annotations that are not in the new JSON line are removed from the dataset. For more information, see Defining JSON lines for anomaly classification in the Amazon Lookout for Vision Developer Guide. The images you reference in the source-ref field of a JSON line, must be in the same S3 bucket as the existing images in the dataset. Updating a dataset might take a while to complete. To check the current status, call DescribeDataset and check the Status field in the response. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:UpdateDatasetEntries operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Add or update dataset JSON Line entries
data.description_suggestion: Adds or updates one or more JSON Line entries in a dataset for model training or testing. Use the source-ref field to identify existing entries for updates; the supplied JSON line replaces the existing entry and removes any annotations not included in the new entry. All referenced images must be in the same S3 bucket as existing dataset images. Dataset updates may take time to complete; use DescribeDataset to check the Status field for completion. Returns a 202 Accepted response indicating the update is in progress. Requires lookoutvision:UpdateDatasetEntries permission.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'UpdateDatasetEntries' is not action-oriented. Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity about the response (202 Accepted) and could better emphasize the replacement behavior and annotation removal side effects for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Adds one or more key-value tags to an Amazon Lookout for Vision model. For more information, see Tagging a model in the Amazon Lookout for Vision Developer Guide. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:TagResource operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Add tags to a resource
data.description_suggestion: Adds one or more key-value tags to an Amazon Lookout for Vision resource (model, project, or dataset). Tags are used for resource organization, cost allocation, and metadata management. Returns a 200 OK response on successful tag addition. If a tag key already exists, its value is updated. Requires lookoutvision:TagResource permission.
data.reasoning: Original summary is not action-oriented. Description lacks clarity about resource types, return value, and behavior when tags already exist. Enhanced version specifies supported resource types, explains tag update behavior, and clarifies the response for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Removes one or more tags from an Amazon Lookout for Vision model. For more information, see Tagging a model in the Amazon Lookout for Vision Developer Guide. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:UntagResource operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Remove tags from a resource
data.description_suggestion: Removes one or more specified tags from an Amazon Lookout for Vision resource (model, project, or dataset) by tag key. Only the specified tag keys are removed; other tags remain unchanged. Returns a 200 OK response on successful removal. Attempting to remove non-existent tags does not cause an error. Requires lookoutvision:UntagResource permission.
data.reasoning: Original summary is not action-oriented. Description lacks clarity about resource types, what gets removed (tag keys vs values), return value, and behavior with non-existent tags. Enhanced version clarifies these details and explains idempotent behavior for AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'startModel' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Original summary lacks clarity about the operation's purpose (hosting for inference). Description is comprehensive but doesn't explicitly state the response code (202 Accepted) or clarify that billing starts when HOSTED. Enhanced version emphasizes inference capability, clarifies asynchronous nature, and specifies response code for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Starts the running of the version of an Amazon Lookout for Vision model. Starting a model takes a while to complete. To check the current state of the model, use DescribeModel. A model is ready to use when its status is HOSTED. Once the model is running, you can detect custom labels in new images by calling DetectAnomalies. You are charged for the amount of time that the model is running. To stop a running model, call StopModel. This operation requires permissions to perform the lookoutvision:StartModel operation.
data.summary_suggestion: Start hosting a model for inference
data.description_suggestion: Starts hosting the specified model version to enable real-time anomaly detection on new images. The model transitions to HOSTED status when ready for inference. Starting a model is asynchronous; use DescribeModel to check the current status. Once HOSTED, call DetectAnomalies to identify defects in images. Returns a 202 Accepted response indicating the start request is in progress. Billing begins when the model reaches HOSTED status. Use StopModel to stop hosting and cease billing. Requires lookoutvision:StartModel permission.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks clarity about the operation's purpose (hosting for inference). Description is comprehensive but doesn't explicitly state the response code (202 Accepted) or clarify that billing starts when HOSTED. Enhanced version emphasizes inference capability, clarifies asynchronous nature, and specifies response code for better AI agent understanding.
